root_text.an5.85:1.1: “Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato thero bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ appiyo ca hoti amanāpo ca agaru ca abhāvanīyo ca.
root_text.an5.85:1.3: Akkhamo hoti rūpānaṁ, akkhamo saddānaṁ, akkhamo gandhānaṁ, akkhamo rasānaṁ, akkhamo phoṭṭhabbānaṁ—
root_text.an5.85:1.4: imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato thero bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ appiyo ca hoti amanāpo ca agaru ca abhāvanīyo ca.
root_text.an5.85:2.1: Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato thero bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ piyo ca hoti manāpo ca garu ca bhāvanīyo ca.
root_text.an5.85:2.3: Khamo hoti rūpānaṁ, khamo saddānaṁ, khamo gandhānaṁ, khamo rasānaṁ, khamo phoṭṭhabbānaṁ—
root_text.an5.85:2.4: imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato thero bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ piyo ca hoti manāpo ca garu ca bhāvanīyo cā”ti.

translation_text.an5.85:1.1: “Mendicants, a senior mendicant with five qualities is unlikable and disagreeable to their spiritual companions, not respected or admired.
translation_text.an5.85:1.3: They can’t endure sights, sounds, smells, tastes, and touches.
translation_text.an5.85:1.4: A senior mendicant with these five qualities is unlikable and disagreeable to their spiritual companions, not respected or admired.
translation_text.an5.85:2.1: A senior mendicant with five qualities is dear and beloved to their spiritual companions, respected and admired.
translation_text.an5.85:2.3: They can endure sights, sounds, smells, tastes, and touches.
translation_text.an5.85:2.4: A senior mendicant with these five qualities is dear and beloved to their spiritual companions, respected and admired.”
